sections.title: ATV tier now renders artifacts as designed documents,
sections.sub: Six section kinds, one opinionated dark template, zero CDN dependencies. Every artifact emitted from this tier carries the same visual language used internally at design-driven shops, generated programmatically from a structured input contract.

sections.title: Render pipeline health.
sections.lede: End-to-end view of the render pipeline. Each row is a phase, with the live status of its acceptance test.

sections.title: Section kinds the renderer understands.
sections.lede: Each section is emitted by a small dedicated function that maps an input dict to opinionated HTML. The CSS in atv-tier.html.j2 owns the rest.

rows.name: hero
rows.why: Top-of-page eyebrow, headline, sub, meta strip. One per artifact.

rows.name: sec
rows.why: Numbered section head with optional aside pill. Wraps inner sections.

rows.name: stack-list
rows.why: Three-column hairline list. Best for "item / why / fix" rows.

rows.name: dep-list
rows.why: Four-column list with role + version. Best for dependencies and audits.

rows.name: q-list
rows.why: Numbered question-and-answer rows. Best for intake checklists.

rows.name: steps
rows.why: Vertical timeline with dot markers. Best for sequential procedures.

rows.name: code-shell
rows.why: macOS-style code block with traffic dots + language tag.

rows.name: status-table
rows.why: Typed table with auto status badges (DONE/PASS green, FAIL red, IN_PROGRESS indigo).

rows.name: color-strip
rows.why: Six-cell palette swatches with hex + name + role.

rows.name: fit-row
rows.why: Pill chips with green/rose dots for fit / avoid signals.

rows.name: anti
rows.why: Anti-pattern block with DO NOT mono label and bullet list.

rows.name: checklist
rows.why: Single-column hairline-separated list with mono · markers.

rows.name: props-table
rows.why: Classic prop/type/default/notes table for API surfaces.

rows.name: subhead
rows.why: Mono uppercase label inside a section. For sub-grouping.

sections.title: Tokens consumed by the atv tier.
sections.lede: These are the tokens declared in designs/atv.DESIGN.md. Every other color in the rendered output is derived (semi-transparent overlays, hairline borders, glow ambients).

colors.name: Background
colors.role: Page canvas, near-black with a hint of blue.

colors.name: Surface
colors.role: Container fill for cards, lists, tables.

colors.name: Rose
colors.role: Counter-accent for status dots, never fills.

colors.name: Success
colors.role: Dot indicators on fit chips and topbar tag.

colors.name: Danger
colors.role: Dot indicators on avoid chips and fail badges.

sections.title: Input contract.
sections.lede: The atv tier reads the same input shape as the legacy tiers, with one addition: a sections: […] array. When present, the section emitters take over and the legacy rows / body_md paths are bypassed.

rows.name: title
rows.notes: Sets <title> and slug. Hero section owns the visible H1.

rows.name: sections
rows.notes: Rich section graph. When set, takes precedence over rows/body_md.

sections.title: How a section is declared.
sections.lede: The same shape across every section: a kind discriminator plus kind-specific fields.

sections.title: Design intent.
sections.lede: These rules are not enforced by code. They are how the design holds together.

items: Exactly one hero per artifact, at the top.
Number every section. 00 through NN, two digits.
Lede paragraphs no longer than three sentences.
Avoid filling more than 15% of the surface with accent or rose.
No emojis in section heads. Mono eyebrows only.
Code shells get a file-path marker when the code targets a specific file.
Do not introduce a light theme inside an atv-tier artifact.
Do not stack two hero sections.
Do not use status-table for non-status data — the badges will mislead.
Do not nest sec inside sec beyond two levels.
Do not add custom CSS to the rendered HTML. Edit the template.

sections.title: Acceptance checks.
sections.lede: Pre-merge gate for the v0.2.0 release.

items: Headless Chrome screenshot of this artifact shows the dark canvas (rgb(8,9,10)).
Hero H1 is visible in white at the top of the file.
Status badges in section 00 are colored (green / indigo) not gray.
Color strip in section 02 shows six distinct swatches.
